task.title: Reject empty event-type discriminators before storage
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/366 by preventing the typed event log from persisting an empty compatibility discriminator.
acceptance_criteria: An event whose event type is empty returns a dedicated EventLogError and writes no row.
The rejected stream replays as empty.
Non-empty event-type strings remain opaque and existing append and replay behavior remains unchanged.
The public event-log documentation identifies the validation boundary and stable error.
The pinned local and required pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added focused integration coverage for appending an event with an empty event-type discriminator.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test failed to compile because EventLogError had no InvalidEventType variant, confirming that the append API did not expose the required rejection behavior.
attempts.patch: Added an EmptyEventType test fixture and asserted a dedicated error plus an empty replay result without changing production behavior.

attempts.summary: Validated the discriminator at the start of append and returned a dedicated public error.
attempts.patch: Captured Event::event_type once, rejected the empty string before payload validation, encoding, or transaction creation, and reused the validated value for insertion.

attempts.summary: Kept discriminator policy minimal and documented the compatibility surface.
attempts.diagnostic: A format policy or database migration would have exceeded the requirement; the event type remains opaque except for the non-empty invariant.
attempts.patch: Added EventLogError::InvalidEventType with no wrapped source and updated docs/event-log.md with the append boundary and no-write contract.

attempts.summary: Ran focused and full verification, then obtained independent pre-commit and automated pull-request review.
attempts.diagnostic: The independent Codex review found no blocking findings. The GitHub Rust quality gate passed, and CodeRabbit generated no actionable comments with all five pre-merge checks passing.
attempts.patch: No corrective patch was required after review.

outcome.summary: The typed append boundary now rejects empty event-type discriminators before any encoding or write transaction while preserving opaque non-empty discriminators and existing replay behavior.

verification.command: /run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test event_log rejects_empty_event_type_without_writing -- --exact
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command codex review --uncommitted # result: no blocking findings
GitHub Rust quality gate for pull request 367
CodeRabbit review for pull request 367 # result: no actionable comments and five pre-merge checks passed
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0011-empty-event-type-validation.json # result: valid development record
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 11 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Validate persistence discriminators at the append boundary so invalid compatibility metadata never reaches encoding or storage.
A dedicated domain error distinguishes caller invariant failures from serialization and database failures.
Read trait-provided persistence metadata once, validate it, and reuse the same value to avoid disagreement between validation and insertion.
Prefer the narrow non-empty invariant over an unrequested naming policy or storage migration.
